title: Marque Applied Fix (Audit Record)
description: An audit record emitted for every applied fix. Serializes the AppliedFix type from data-model.md. Streamed as NDJSON on stderr. Sub-threshold suggestions (FixProposal without promotion) never appear in this stream — they are carried only in the diagnostic stream. Contract version: marque-mvp-1.

schema.description: Contract version of this audit record. Present on every record (including error frames) so downstream consumers can detect version drift without out-of-band metadata. Bumped on any breaking change to the audit record shape.

source.description: Provenance of the originating FixProposal. `BuiltinRule` = hand-written Layer 2 CAPCO rule; `CorrectionsMap` = user-supplied `[corrections]` entry from .marque.toml (FR-009); `MigrationTable` = deterministic deprecated-marking conversion from the generated migration table (FR-004a). Compliance reviewers use this to classify changes without string-matching on `migration_ref`.

rule.description: Stable rule identifier of the rule that produced the originating FixProposal.

span.description: Byte range in the original input buffer that was (or would be) replaced.

original.description: The exact original text covered by the fix span.

replacement.description: The text that replaced (or would replace, in dry-run) the original.

confidence.description: The confidence score of the originating FixProposal. Always >= configuration.confidence_threshold (sub-threshold proposals are never promoted to an AppliedFix).

migration_ref.description: Optional stable reference to a migration-table entry or corrections-map source (e.g. 'corrections-map', 'ism-2022-dec-deprecated-dissem'). Null if the fix is not sourced from a table.

timestamp.description: RFC 3339 / ISO 8601 timestamp of AppliedFix construction, in UTC. Produced by the Engine's injected Clock — tests use a fixed clock for deterministic snapshots.

classifier_id.description: Classifier identity from MARQUE_CLASSIFIER_ID or .marque.local.toml. NEVER populated from a committed config file. Null if no identity was configured.

dry_run.description: True iff the AppliedFix was produced under --dry-run (no bytes written to disk). The NDJSON stream is otherwise byte-identical between a dry-run and a real run, satisfying FR-006.

input.description: Identifier of the input the fix applies to (file path, '-' for stdin, or null when not applicable). Set by the CLI caller (or embedder) at the engine boundary; not populated by rules. Corresponds to `AppliedFix.input` in data-model.md.
